name: D+Q bone RCT 20-week BMD synthesis compatibility
question: Can the 20-week BMD result from the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT be pooled for a meta-analysis estimate?

compatibility_basis.population: Postmenopausal women in the D+Q and untreated-control arms of one phase 2 randomized trial.
compatibility_basis.endpoint_family: DXA bone mineral density percent change by lumbar spine, femoral neck, and radius site.
compatibility_basis.effect_metric: Site-specific between-group BMD percent-change effect with uncertainty.
compatibility_basis.variance_model: No pooling model selected because the registry record is descriptive, site-combined, and lacks pairwise site-specific effects with uncertainty.

pooling_rationale: Pooling is blocked by result count and descriptive-only extraction. The BMD record contains site-stratified group medians and full ranges, but no posted pairwise D+Q versus control analysis for an overall BMD endpoint and no site-specific effect values or uncertainty estimates. Lumbar spine, femoral neck, and radius values should not be collapsed into one pooled effect without site-specific outcome definitions.
non_pooling_reason: Do not pool BMD until site-specific outcomes or a pre-specified composite BMD metric are extracted with effect values, uncertainty, comparison, sample size, and group-value fields from at least two compatible results.

missing_effect_fields_by_result.note: The result is descriptive-only: it has site-stratified group summaries but no pairwise overall or site-specific D+Q effect value with variance-compatible uncertainty.

self_healing_actions: Keep BMD pooling blocked until site-specific effect records are extracted or a defensible composite BMD outcome is defined.
Route BMD extraction to extraction_refresh if full text or supplements provide site-level pairwise estimates with uncertainty.

provenance.note: Posted BMD group values by lumbar spine, femoral neck, and radius classes.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract exploratory BMD subgroup sentence
provenance.note: Abstract mentions an exploratory radius BMD subgroup signal, which is not the same as the overall registry-posted BMD summary.
